explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: The Texas Instruments Edge AI Technology Page describes governance over the AI model lifecycle. This page also references AI tools and ecosystems for development, deployment, and management, and describes the company's AI product portfolio, including processors and vision processors.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The Texas Instruments Edge AI Technology Page provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. This company-owned page describes TI's AI product portfolio, including processors and vision processors, and mentions AI tools and ecosystems for development, deployment, and management. These descriptions imply governance over the AI model lifecycle and transparency in the development and deployment processes.
relevant_sources.title: Texas Instruments Edge AI Technology Page

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: The Texas Instruments Edge AI Technology Page describes transparency in the development and deployment processes. This page also references AI tools and ecosystems for development, deployment, and management, and describes the company's AI product portfolio, including processors and vision processors.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: The Texas Instruments Edge AI Technology Page provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. This company-owned page describes TI's AI product portfolio, including processors and vision processors, and mentions AI tools and ecosystems for development, deployment, and management. These descriptions imply governance over the AI model lifecycle and transparency in the development and deployment processes.
relevant_sources.title: Texas Instruments Edge AI Technology Page

summary.overall_findings: Texas Instruments' public disclosures address transparency in the development and deployment processes, as well as governance over the AI model lifecycle. These two pillars are the only ones with documented evidence out of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Drawing from 14 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
